reasoning_model_1: The model completely failed to follow the core instructions. Firstly, it generated speech in Chinese ('咳嗽，我刚才呛到了，有点不舒服') instead of the requested English text ('I just choked, it's a bit uncomfortable.'). Secondly, regarding the required paralinguistic feature (Non-Linguistic Vocalizations), the model verbally spoke the Chinese word for 'cough' ('咳嗽' at 00:00) instead of generating an actual coughing sound. Due to the wrong language and the failure to perform the non-linguistic vocalization, the audio is scored as completely incorrect.
reasoning_model_2: The model successfully executed the instruction. It begins with a realistic non-linguistic vocalization—a coughing/throat-clearing sound (00:00-00:01)—which perfectly satisfies the 'coughing' requirement. The spoken content ('I just choked, it is a bit uncomfortable') is accurate to the script, with a natural delivery that incorporates pauses and a tone consistent with the physical sensation of having just choked. The integration of the effect with the speech is fluid and natural.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 failed fundamentally by speaking in the wrong language (Chinese instead of English) and reading the instruction ('cough') aloud rather than performing the sound effect. Model 2 correctly spoke the English text and successfully integrated a realistic coughing sound at the beginning, demonstrating excellent control over non-linguistic vocalizations and context.
